name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 27.01.2026

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 27.01.2026.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Sowing Status: As on January 23, 2026, the area sown under Rabi crops is 660.48 lakh ha (including 89.36 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 95.88 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 642.24 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rabi Sowing Status: As on January 23, 2026, the area sown under Rabi crops is 660.48 lakh ha (including 89.36 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 95.88 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 642.24 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on 23.01.2026, 99% of area has been harvested for Kharif crops all over India .
texts.text:  As on 23.01.2026, 99% of area has been harvested for Kharif crops all over India .

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Minimum  temperatures  are likely to  be  below  normal  in  Central  India, East India, many parts of Maharashtra and many parts of South India. TMIN is likely to be  above  normal  in  many  parts  of  NW  India,  Saurashtra-Kutch,  Madhya  Maharashtra, some parts of South Karnataka and NE India.
texts.text: Temperature: Minimum  temperatures  are likely to  be  below  normal  in  Central  India, East India, many parts of Maharashtra and many parts of South India. TMIN is likely to be  above  normal  in  many  parts  of  NW  India,  Saurashtra-Kutch,  Madhya  Maharashtra, some parts of South Karnataka and NE India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall Distribution: From January 01- January 26 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is  39%  lower  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast  India:  (-)  89%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  (-)  21%;  (iii)  Central  India:  (-)  99%;  and South Peninsula: 21%.
texts.text: Rainfall Distribution: From January 01- January 26 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is  39%  lower  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast  India:  (-)  89%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  (-)  21%;  (iii)  Central  India:  (-)  99%;  and South Peninsula: 21%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  A  fresh  western  disturbance  is  likely  to  affect  northwest  India  from  the  night  of  30th January 2026.
texts.text:  A  fresh  western  disturbance  is  likely  to  affect  northwest  India  from  the  night  of  30th January 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  An intense Western Disturbance likely to cause light/moderate rainfall/snowfall at many places over Western Himalayan region during 26 th -28 th and isolated heavy rainfall/snowfall and hailstorm on 27 th  January, 2026.
texts.text:  An intense Western Disturbance likely to cause light/moderate rainfall/snowfall at many places over Western Himalayan region during 26 th -28 th and isolated heavy rainfall/snowfall and hailstorm on 27 th  January, 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Light/moderate  rainfall  likely  at  some  places  over  adjoining  plains  of  northwest  India along with isolated thunderstorms/lightning/hailstorm and winds speed reaching 40-50 kmph gusting to 60 kmph on 27 th  January, 2026.
texts.text:  Light/moderate  rainfall  likely  at  some  places  over  adjoining  plains  of  northwest  India along with isolated thunderstorms/lightning/hailstorm and winds speed reaching 40-50 kmph gusting to 60 kmph on 27 th  January, 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated/Scattered  rainfall  activity  likely  over  Punjab,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Tamil  Nadu  and Madhya Pradesh.
texts.text:  Isolated/Scattered  rainfall  activity  likely  over  Punjab,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Tamil  Nadu  and Madhya Pradesh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be below normal over the country except the Western Himalayan Region and Northwest India, where it is likely  to be  near  normal to  above normal.
texts.text:  Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be below normal over the country except the Western Himalayan Region and Northwest India, where it is likely  to be  near  normal to  above normal.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Dense  fog  conditions  also  likely  during  morning/night  hours  in  isolated  pockets  over Uttarakhand till 27th; Himachal Pradesh during 28th-31st; Uttar Pradesh on 27th, 29th & 30th; Rajasthan during 28th-30th & Bihar during 29th-31st January.
texts.text:  Dense  fog  conditions  also  likely  during  morning/night  hours  in  isolated  pockets  over Uttarakhand till 27th; Himachal Pradesh during 28th-31st; Uttar Pradesh on 27th, 29th & 30th; Rajasthan during 28th-30th & Bihar during 29th-31st January.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Cold wave conditions likely in isolated pockets over Himachal Pradesh during 29th-31st; Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh during 28th-31st January.
texts.text:  Cold wave conditions likely in isolated pockets over Himachal Pradesh during 29th-31st; Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh during 28th-31st January.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water Reservoir Status : As on 22 January 2026, the total live water storage in 166 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 130.753 billion cubic meter, which is 71.2%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's  storage  is  nearly 107.10 percent of last year's storage and 123.50 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water Reservoir Status : As on 22 January 2026, the total live water storage in 166 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 130.753 billion cubic meter, which is 71.2%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's  storage  is  nearly 107.10 percent of last year's storage and 123.50 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels :  In  August  2025,  In  Post-monsoon  2025, around  90% of the country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper  water  levels  of  more  than  20  m  are  observed  in  around  5%  of  stations  of  the country  covering  mainly  the  western  states,  especially  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater Levels :  In  August  2025,  In  Post-monsoon  2025, around  90% of the country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper  water  levels  of  more  than  20  m  are  observed  in  around  5%  of  stations  of  the country  covering  mainly  the  western  states,  especially  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh  during  Post-monsoon  2025  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.
texts.text:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh  during  Post-monsoon  2025  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Annual  water  level  comparison  with  the  previous  year  Post-monsoon  2024  to  Postmonsoon  2025  has  shown  that  about  63%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country experienced rise in  annual  water level fluctuation may  be  attributed to the less groundwater extraction compared to 2024.
texts.text:  Annual  water  level  comparison  with  the  previous  year  Post-monsoon  2024  to  Postmonsoon  2025  has  shown  that  about  63%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country experienced rise in  annual  water level fluctuation may  be  attributed to the less groundwater extraction compared to 2024.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 73% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  Postmonsoon 2025.
texts.text:  About 73% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  Postmonsoon 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in Post-monsoon  2025 ,  groundwater conditions  across  India  remained  largely  stable,  with 91%  of  monitoring  stations showing no  deficit .  However,  localized  deficits  were  observed  in  several  regions  notably in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and the Dadar Nagar Havili & Daman & Diu.
texts.text:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in Post-monsoon  2025 ,  groundwater conditions  across  India  remained  largely  stable,  with 91%  of  monitoring  stations showing no  deficit .  However,  localized  deficits  were  observed  in  several  regions  notably in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and the Dadar Nagar Havili & Daman & Diu.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Fertilizers : The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers : The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.text:  During the week (15 Jan - 21 Jan 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 97%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Jan to 21 Jan 2026) is lower than LPA by 82%.

texts.text:  Minimum temperatures are likely to be above normal 1-3°C over most parts of the northwest, west, central and northeast India. It is likely to be below normal 2-4°C over East India; near normal or slightly below normal (by 1-2°C) over rest parts of the country.
